title: Caregiver Starter Checklist
summary: For someone just stepping into a caregiving role — the foundational tasks that make everything after this easier.

tasks.title: Write down what you've noticed
tasks.explanation: Specific changes, with rough dates — this becomes useful for both family conversations and doctors.
tasks.documents: None yet — just a running note.

tasks.title: Have an initial conversation with your parent
tasks.explanation: A calm, low-pressure check-in, not a big "we need to talk."
tasks.notes: See "Talking with Aging Parents" article for approach.

tasks.title: Identify who else is involved
tasks.explanation: Siblings, a spouse, close friends who already help informally.

tasks.title: Locate key documents
tasks.explanation: Power of attorney, health care proxy, insurance cards, ID.
tasks.jurisdictionNote: POA requirements vary by state — confirm any existing document is durable and still valid.
tasks.documents: POA, health care proxy, insurance cards, photo ID.

tasks.title: Build a current medication list
tasks.explanation: Name, dose, timing, prescribing doctor for each.

tasks.title: List current doctors and specialists
tasks.explanation: Name, specialty, phone number, last visit date.
tasks.who: N/A — compiled from memory, mail, or the primary doctor's office.

tasks.title: Take the Care Readiness Assessment
tasks.explanation: Turns scattered observations into a clearer starting picture.

tasks.title: Hold a first family meeting
tasks.explanation: Even informal — who can help with what, going forward.

tasks.title: Take the Caregiver Stress Self-Check yourself
tasks.explanation: Starting this role sustainably matters as much as starting it well.
